stories.text: As an operator tailing the server log, I want a workflow-process crash to print an error-level line with the workflow id, type, and the VM error message the moment it happens so that I never mistake a dead run for a slow one.

As an operator, I want a workflow that times out to log at error like a crash does so that a terminal failure is loud regardless of which terminal it reached, given the engine imposes no default timeout of its own.

As an operator, I want the failure logged even when the failing process ran on a remote worker the server never hosted so that I can rely on the one log surface I already watch rather than chasing per-worker logs.

As an operator, I want a standalone validate endpoint and CLI subcommand I can use to check whether an input payload conforms to a workflow type's schema so that I can verify inputs programmatically before dispatch or as part of a pre-deploy check.

As an operator investigating a provision that took an unexpected branch suffix, I want the collision report to name the specific branch, session file path, and worktree directory that were found pre-existing so that I can clean up stale artifacts without trial and error.

personas.name: AI Team Member
personas.role: Dispatching and monitoring a wave of workflows

stories.text: As an AI team member who dispatched a parent run with children, I want a child's failure to show up loudly — on the parent's timeline and in the log — so that I notice a failed child fast instead of waiting on a parent that will never converge.
As an AI orchestrator watching the event stream, I want the parent-side child-failure event delivered to my subscription so that I can react to a failed child without polling describe.

As an AI team member dispatching a workflow, I want the CLI start command to validate my input JSON against the workflow type's schema before starting so that I discover input errors immediately instead of watching the workflow fail mid-flight on a decode error.

As an AI team member dispatching a stacked-dev workflow, I want the worker to detect and report pre-existing branch, norn session, and worktree collisions before provisioning so that I know upfront when a workspace will conflict with artifacts from a previous run.

personas.name: AI Lead
personas.role: Reviewing a run after a reported failure

stories.text: As an AI lead reviewing why a run died, I want the parent's describe timeline to carry the child's id and its failure reason so that I can see which child failed and why without reconstructing it from separate logs.

As an AI lead, I want the failure announcement to be derived from the recorded terminal event rather than a separately stored status so that the log I read and the durable history I audit can never disagree.

As an AI lead debugging a dispatch error, I want the validation error to include the field path and a description of what the schema expected versus what was provided so that I can fix the input without reverse-engineering the codec or reading the raw schema.

personas.name: Future Maintainer
personas.role: Reading the failure-handling code months later

stories.text: As a future maintainer, I want every workflow-process exit to be logged at one funnel so that I can trust that adding a new way to start a workflow cannot reintroduce a silent-exit path.
As a future maintainer, I want the logging to be a pure side effect that never alters what is recorded or returned so that I can reason about failure handling and observability independently.

As a future maintainer, I want the collision detection to be a separate pre-flight check from the collision handling (attempt-N branch suffixing) so that detection logic can be extended to new resource types without coupling it to the resolution strategy.
